cmd/jobs.purpose: Cobra CLI binary grouping administrative one-off jobs (billing advance/collect, entitlement recalculation, ledger backfill, LLM cost sync, DB migration, quickstart cron). The entire application is wired once via PersistentPreRunE before any sub-command runs; sub-commands source all services from internal.App globals.

patterns.name: PersistentPreRunE DI bootstrap
patterns.description: rootCmd.PersistentPreRunE calls internal.InitializeApplication(ctx, configFileName) before every sub-command. Sub-commands must never initialise services themselves.
patterns.example: PersistentPreRunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error { return internal.InitializeApplication(ctx, configFileName) }

patterns.name: internal.App as the single service registry
patterns.description: All sub-commands access domain services exclusively via package-level globals internal.App and internal.Config — never by constructing services inline.

patterns.name: cmd.Context() for context propagation
patterns.description: Every RunE uses cmd.Context() (signal-aware root context) — never context.Background() or context.TODO().
patterns.example: RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error { return doWork(cmd.Context()) }

patterns.name: Sub-command registration via init() or RootCommand()
patterns.description: Each sub-package exports either a package-level Cmd var registered in init() or a RootCommand() factory. main.go adds them with rootCmd.AddCommand(...).

patterns.name: Nil-guard optional app services before use
patterns.description: Optional features (e.g. ChargesAutoAdvancer) may be nil when the feature is disabled. Always nil-check before calling.

patterns.name: Deferred AppShutdown in main
patterns.description: main.go defers internal.AppShutdown() to release resources regardless of execution path, including early-exit error paths.
patterns.example: defer func() { if internal.AppShutdown != nil { internal.AppShutdown() } }()

patterns.name: Ledger backfill bypasses Wire DI for concrete adapters
patterns.description: When credits.enabled=false Wire provides noop ledger resolvers. The ledger backfill sub-command must construct concrete adapters directly (including lockr.Locker) to write real rows.
patterns.example: // cmd/jobs/ledger/backfillaccounts/service/service.go builds lockr.NewLocker and concrete ledger adapters, not the Wire-injected noops

key_file_guides.role: Binary entry point: signal-aware context, Cobra root, PersistentPreRunE DI bootstrap, sub-command registration, deferred shutdown.
key_file_guides.watch_for: PersistentPreRunE runs before every sub-command — side-effects here affect all commands. AppShutdown must be deferred or resources leak.

key_file_guides.role: Minimal version sub-command; delegates to internal.Version() for ldflags-injected build metadata.
key_file_guides.watch_for: internal.Version() reads ldflags variables — keep in sync with the build toolchain.

key_file_guides.role: Wire provider sets and initializeApplication declaration; source of truth for the DI graph.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding a new Application field requires a matching provider in wire.Build. Never edit wire_gen.go directly — regenerate with 'make generate'.

key_file_guides.role: Package-level App, AppShutdown, Config globals consumed by all sub-packages.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Intentionally global (nolint:gochecknoglobals). Do not introduce a second App construction path.

key_file_guides.role: Pure aggregator — registers advance, advancecharges, collect, subscriptionsync sub-commands with no logic.
key_file_guides.watch_for: No business logic belongs here; keep it as a pure AddCommand aggregator. Each sub-command lives in its own package.

key_file_guides.role: Concrete ledger adapter construction that bypasses DI noops when credits.enabled=false; uses cursor pagination with MAX_SAFE_ITER guard.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Must build lockr.Locker and concrete ledger adapters directly. Using Wire-provided noop resolvers silently skips all writes.

key_file_guides.role: gocron-based polling loops for quickstart/demo environments only.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Not for production workers — production uses Kafka/Watermill. Nil-check all optional services before scheduling.

anti_patterns: Constructing domain services (billing, charges, ledger) directly inside RunE instead of sourcing from internal.App
Using context.Background() or context.TODO() in RunE — always use cmd.Context()
Registering sub-command-specific flags on a parent aggregator Cmd (pollutes sibling flag sets)
Calling tools/migrate DDL or data transforms inline in migrate.go — those belong in tools/migrate/migrations/
Using quickstart gocron polling patterns in production workers — production billing flows use Kafka/Watermill

decisions.decision: PersistentPreRunE wires the entire application once before any sub-command runs
decisions.rationale: Centralises DI bootstrap in main.go so every sub-command automatically gets a fully-wired app without duplicating initialisation logic across sub-packages.

decisions.decision: internal.App package-level globals rather than Cobra context injection
decisions.rationale: Simpler sub-package access pattern; avoids type-assertion boilerplate across many independent sub-packages that each need to pull services from the context.

decisions.decision: Ledger backfill bypasses Wire DI to construct concrete adapters
decisions.rationale: When credits.enabled=false, Wire provides noop ledger resolvers; the backfill job must write real rows so it builds lockr.Locker and concrete adapters directly.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new administrative sub-command that uses a domain service from internal.App
